jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga

Are you ready to join Connecticut Innovation’s vibrant community of innovators? Connecticut Innovations (“CI”) is Connecticut’s strategic venture capital arm, and we are passionate about serving our portfolio of 220+ companies across various industries, with strengths in life sciences, technology, and climate tech.


Come join Jona, a Connecticut Innovations portfolio company!


ABOUT JONA

Every aspect of our health has been shown to be affected by the gut microbiome. However, the complexity of the gut microbiome and the complexity of microbiome science requires advanced AI technology to truly analyze and harness personal gut microbiome data for health.

At Jona, we envision a world where microbiome testing is more common than bloodwork. Our mission is to create that world by using AI to reveal the relationship between the microbiome and health to guide individuals, clinicians and innovators toward discovery and action.


ABOUT THE ROLE

We’re looking for a Senior Software Engineer to join our growing team and play a key role in building reliable, performant, and scalable systems across our product. You’ll work across the stack—from TypeScript APIs to modern React frontends—helping define architecture, improve developer experience, and drive code quality across the team.


In this role, you’ll be expected to work closely with product, design, and fellow engineers to deliver user-facing features, refine our internal APIs, and evolve our platform architecture as we scale. You will see projects through from conception to production and everything in between.


This is a remote role with the opportunity for monthly meetups with the team in New York City.


ROLE OUTLINE

Drive Technical Decisions
  • Propose, architect, and implement projects from the ground up.
  • Design, implement, and maintain scalable systems and APIs.
  • Drive modernization of legacy systems and ensure adherence to best practices.

Collaborate & Communicate
  • Work directly with other engineers, designers, and product management to efficiently deliver high-quality features for both external and internal audiences.
  • Build, test, and deploy new features across frontend and backend systems.
  • Ensure performant, accessible, and maintainable code on both client and server sides.

Take Ownership & Propose Improvements
  • Don’t simply write code, own it all the way to production.
  • Continually improve our software delivery process.
  • Stay current with emerging technologies and frameworks, evaluating their fit for the organization.
  • Propose and lead initiatives that improve developer experience, CI/CD pipelines, and internal tooling.
  • Identify and resolve performance bottlenecks across frontend and backend services.


WHAT YOU’LL BRING
  • 5+ years of professional experience building modern TypeScript web applications
  • Deep understanding of React and Node.js
  • Experience designing and integrating with RESTful and RPC-style APIs
  • Strong knowledge of SQL (PostgreSQL preferred) and data modeling best practices
  • Understand relational and non-relational databases
  • Familiarity with modern CI/CD pipelines (GitHub Actions preferred)
  • Experience building and deploying production workloads on AWS
  • Experience improving codebases through testing, observability, and refactoring
  • Strong communication and collaboration skills, with a product-minded approach
  • An understanding of security issues and responsibilities


NICE TO HAVES
  • Experience working in a regulated environment (HIPAA)
  • Experience with any of the following:
  • Next.js, Nest.js, Effect, Drizzle, Pulumi


WHY JOIN JONA
  • Make a Mark: Help define customer success at a mission-driven, science-backed startup.
  • Grow with Us: Build relationships with leading physicians and health-conscious consumers.
  • Competitive Compensation: Includes salary and meaningful equity.

jobs.title: Web Designer
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga

STAND 8 provides end to end IT solutions to enterprise partners across the United States and with offices in Los Angeles, New York, New Jersey, Atlanta, and more including internationally in Mexico and India.
STAND 8 is seeking two highly skilled Web Designers to support a fast-moving digital design workstream within a large, regulated corporate environment. This role requires experienced designers who can contribute immediately, balancing creativity with structured brand governance and established design systems. The Web Designer will deliver high-quality digital and visual assets while adhering to strict corporate standards and timelines. This is a contract role with a strong possibility of conversion.

Location & Work Type
 Location: Remote, United States
 Work Type: Remote

Key Responsibilities

  • Design and update web experiences using Figma, leveraging existing design systems, templates, and component libraries.

  • Apply and maintain strict adherence to corporate brand standards, including typography, color systems, layout rules, and accessibility requirements.

  • Collaborate with content and delivery teams to ensure designs are optimized for platform implementation.

  • Create, edit, and optimize visual assets, including image cropping and color correction, using Adobe Photoshop.

  • Develop supporting graphics and materials using Illustrator, InDesign, and PowerPoint.

  • Execute assigned tasks independently while managing priorities in a structured, delivery-focused environment.

  • Incorporate feedback efficiently from creative leadership and stakeholders.

  • Communicate clearly and professionally with cross-functional partners across multiple time zones.

Qualifications
 Required:

  • Proven experience as a Web Designer within enterprise or corporate environments.

  • Advanced proficiency in Figma with the ability to contribute immediately.

  • Extensive experience with Adobe Creative Suite, including Photoshop, Illustrator, and InDesign.

  • Strong graphic design fundamentals, including layout, typography, and color theory.

  • Experience producing professional presentation materials using PowerPoint.

  • Demonstrated ability to work within strict brand guidelines and structured design systems.

  • Strong attention to detail and excellent visual judgment.

  • Ability to work remotely while aligning with UK or EST business hours, including an 8:00 AM EST start time if applicable.

Preferred (Optional):

  • Familiarity with Adobe Experience Manager (AEM) and designing for AEM-based websites.

  • Experience working in regulated or highly structured corporate environments.

  • Experience using project and task management tools such as Jira or Monday.com.

  • Background supporting financial or enterprise-level brands.

Benefits
  • Medical coverage and Health Savings Account (HSA) through Anthem
  • Dental/Vision/Various Ancillary coverages through Unum
  • 401(k) retirement savings plan
  • Paid-time-off options
  • Company-paid Employee Assistance Program (EAP)
  • Discount programs through ADP WorkforceNow

Additional Details
 The base range for this contract position is $35.83 - $45.83 / per hour, depending on experience. Our pay ranges are determined by role, level, and location. The range displayed on each job posting reflects the minimum and maximum target for new hires of this position across all US locations. Within the range, individual pay is determined by work location and additional factors, including job-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training. Qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records will be considered.

About Us
 STAND 8 provides end-to-end IT solutions to enterprise partners across the United States and globally with offices in Los Angeles, Atlanta, New York, Mexico, Japan, India, and more. STAND 8 focuses on the "bleeding edge" of technology and leverages automation, process, marketing, and over fifteen years of success and growth to provide a world-class experience for our customers, partners, and employees.
 Our mission is to impact the world positively by creating success through PEOPLE, PROCESS, and TECHNOLOGY.
 Check out more at www.stand8.io; and reach out today to explore opportunities to grow together!
 By applying to this position, your data will be processed in accordance with the STAND 8 Privacy Policy.

jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga

Tebra only initiates contact with candidates via email from an official Tebra email address (@tebra.com, @patientpop.com, or @kareo.com) or through our applicant tracking system, Greenhouse. We will only ask you to provide sensitive personal information through our official application portal — not via social media or text message. We do not conduct interviews via instant messaging.

About The Role

As a Senior Full Stack Engineer, you will primarily design and build backend services while possessing the adaptability to support frontend development when necessary. You will evolve the core API layer and downstream services, delivering secure, performant REST APIs. You will lead technical design reviews, improve service reliability, and collaborate across the stack to ship features end-to-end. While your focus is on the backend, you may step into frontend codebases (Angular/React) to unblock delivery and ensure a cohesive user experience.

Your Area of Focus

  • Own and evolve platform APIs and services, ensuring low-latency, resilient endpoints for client applications.
  • Design and implement Spring Boot microservices using modern patterns (contract-first APIs, layered architecture, DTO mappers, caching).
  • Improve reliability and performance with caching (e.g. Redis, etc.), connection management, and efficient data access.
  • Contribute to frontend development using React and TypeScript, building modern, maintainable user interfaces and collaborating across the stack to support feature delivery and integration.

Professional Qualifications
  • 5+ years of software development experience building Java services in production.
  • Expertise with Spring Boot and REST API design; comfortable with contract-first development and layered service architectures.
  • Experience building and consuming service/domain contracts and integrating external/partner APIs.
  • Proficient with data modeling and access patterns; capable of optimizing queries, caching, and serialization for high-performance applications.
  • Proficient in automated testing: unit, integration, component, contract, and performance testing.
  • Strong focus on observability, performance profiling, and resilience (timeouts, retries, circuit breakers, idempotency).
  • Strong verbal and written communication skills; able to explain design tradeoffs to technical and non-technical partners.
  • Familiarity with AI-assisted development tools (e.g., Codex CLI) to improve engineering productivity and code quality.

(For Recruiter use only)

We are dedicated to attracting and retaining top talent with competitive and fair compensation. For this position, this range reflects our Zone 1 (National Average) pay band. Your specific compensation is thoughtfully determined by your experience, qualifications, the specific requirements of the role, and your Geo Zone. Our geo-zone system ensures your pay is competitive for your location, recognizing varying costs of labor across regions.Our four geo zones are designed to reflect this:Zone 1: National AverageZone 2: Moderately Higher Cost RegionsZone 3: High-Cost RegionsZone 4: Lower-Cost RegionsBeyond base compensation, Tebra offers eligible employees the opportunity for variable pay and a robust benefits package, reflecting our commitment to your overall well-being. In compliance with California pay transparency laws, the specific compensation range applicable to your Geo Zone will be shared during your initial talent screen.

Zone 1 (National Average): $153,000 USD - $163,000 USD

About Tebra

Kareo and PatientPop have joined forces to become Tebra, the digital backbone for practice well-being. While our teams are still supporting both products, our new hires and current employees are now united as Team Tebra.

Tebra aims to unlock better healthcare by helping independent practices bring modernized care to patients everywhere. Well over 100,000 providers trust Tebra to elevate their patient experience, and help them grow their practice. At Tebra, we’re building the future of well-being together. That shared vision for tomorrow begins with compassion and humanity today.

Our Values

Start with the Customer

We get to know our customers - and their patients - and look at the world through their lens.

Keep It Simple

Healthcare is too complex. We aim to simplify it for everyone.

Stay Entrepreneurial

We reject the status quo and solve problems with creativity, perseverance, and a bias to action.

Better Together

We are diverse, humble, and collaborative. We put the team first and win together.

Celebrate Success

Life is short and joy is underrated. We take time to have fun and celebrate success.

Perks & Benefits

United States: In addition to our healthcare benefits, we also offer amazing perks! Need work from home basics? We offer a discount through Dell! We also offer a number of resources to help you keep your mind and body healthy. Check out Gympass for a great workout, or TelusEmployee Assistance Program to find mental health resources, along with other resources for everyday occurrences.

Costa Rica: To assist with all of life’s needs, Tebra also offers a wellness and childcare subsidy and a University/Education discount! We also offer a number of resources to help you keep your mind and body healthy. Check out Gympass for access to health and fitness apps, or Telus Employee Assistance Program to find mental health resources, along with other resources for everyday occurrences.

Compliance & Privacy Disclosures

NOTE: Tebra is an equal opportunity employer. All applicants will be considered for employment without attention to age, race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, veteran or disability status.

California residents who apply or are recruited for a job with us: please carefully review our California-specific Privacy Notice under the California Consumer Protection Act here: https://www.tebra.com/privacy-policy/california-supplemental-notice/

Sobre a vaga

This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Senior Frontend Developer (React) in Israel.

This is an exciting opportunity for a seasoned frontend developer to build high-impact web applications in a fast-moving, product-focused environment. You will take ownership of developing modern, scalable user interfaces while ensuring performance, reliability, and maintainability. Working closely with product, design, and backend teams, you’ll transform complex ideas into intuitive user experiences. The role emphasizes both engineering excellence and strong testing practices, giving you end-to-end responsibility for quality delivery. You’ll have the chance to work with cutting-edge tools and continuously evolve your technical expertise. This position is ideal for someone who thrives in a collaborative setting, enjoys solving complex problems, and is passionate about delivering exceptional frontend experiences.

Accountabilities

  • Design and develop user-facing features using React, JavaScript, and TypeScript, ensuring high performance and responsiveness
  • Build reusable components and scalable UI libraries to support long-term product growth
  • Translate complex UI/UX designs into clean, efficient, and maintainable code
  • Collaborate cross-functionally with product managers, designers, and backend engineers to deliver seamless solutions
  • Optimize applications for speed, scalability, and technical feasibility across different environments
  • Lead automated testing initiatives, including writing and maintaining test scripts using tools like Cypress, Playwright, or Storybook
  • Conduct code reviews and enforce best practices to maintain high standards of code quality
  • Continuously improve existing codebases and address performance or usability issues proactively

Requirements
  • 5+ years of experience in frontend development with strong proficiency in JavaScript and TypeScript
  • Extensive experience building applications with React and Single-Page Application architectures
  • Solid knowledge of HTML5, CSS3, and modern styling approaches such as SCSS, CSS-in-JS, or TailwindCSS
  • Hands-on experience with GraphQL and tools such as Apollo
  • Proven experience with automated testing and visual regression tools (Cypress, Playwright, Storybook, or similar)
  • Ability to translate design concepts into high-quality, production-ready interfaces
  • Strong problem-solving skills with attention to detail and a commitment to code quality
  • Excellent communication skills and ability to work effectively in a collaborative, remote environment
  • Experience with component libraries such as Ant Design is a plus; familiarity with cloud platforms or data visualization tools is an advantage

Benefits
  • Fully remote work environment with flexible working arrangements
  • Opportunity to work in a fast-growing, innovation-driven tech environment
  • Collaborative and supportive team culture focused on continuous improvement
  • Structured onboarding and ongoing professional development opportunities
  • Career growth potential with exposure to modern technologies and tools
  • Regular team engagement activities and knowledge-sharing sessions
  • Chance to work on meaningful products with real-world impact

Why Apply Through Jobgether?

The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.

We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!

jobs.title: Frontend Engineer – Moderation Tools
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga

Job Title: Frontend Engineer – Moderation Tools

Contract Duration: 6 months (with potential for extension)

Location: Remote (EST)


Must Have Skills:

3-5 years’ experience -Core JavaScript (strong fundamentals, not just framework knowledge)

TypeScript

React — explicitly called out as must-have in technical specs

CSS — semantic, responsive, accessible

GraphQL — query/mutation experience

REST API integration


ENTERPRISE CLIENT:

Nice-to-Have:

  • Lit / Web Components — preferred but teachable
  • GraphQL schema/API design (beyond just consuming)
  • RESTful API design (shaping contracts with backend)
  • Web Components (native, framework-agnostic)
  • SSR / server-side rendering experience
  • Performance optimization for data-heavy UIs
  • Moderation, T&S, or admin tooling background


Job Description:

Overview:

Every day, people submit, vote, and comment on the topics they care most about across 100,000+ active communities and ~100M+ daily active unique visitors worldwide.

The Safety and Moderation organization builds the tools that help moderators keep the client safe, healthy, and scalable. Moderators are community leaders and tastemakers; our job is to give them fast, reliable, and intuitive tooling so they can focus on cultivating great communities instead of fighting the UI.

Within this org, the Moderation Enforcement Tools (MET) team focuses on moderation workflows, enforcement tools, and rule-based automations across clients' new web client.


Role Overview:

We’re looking for a contract frontend engineer with strong experience in modern web development (TypeScript/JavaScript) to help us ship high-impact features and improvements in for moderators. You’ll work closely with other frontend, backend, and mobile engineers, along with product and design partners in the Safety and Moderation organization.

This is a hands-on implementation role focused on web frontend engineering, not general UI prototyping or design-only work.


What You’ll Do:

  • Implement frontend features and enhancements in for moderator tools such as mod queue, mod log, modmail, inline moderation, and rule/automation surfaces.
  • Help drive projects from spec/tickets through implementation, code review, testing, and rollout, collaborating with backend and mobile engineers to deliver end to end solutions for moderators and safety workflows.
  • Contribute to shared frontend architecture and patterns (Lit components, GraphQL usage, CI guardrails, etc.), keeping code maintainable and scalable as we migrate remaining moderation functionality from older web clients.
  • Partner with PMs, designers, and other engineers to clarify requirements, provide technical input, and ensure we’re building tools that truly solve moderator and safety needs.
  • Participate in code reviews and help maintain the overall quality bar for frontend work in Moderation Enforcement.


What We’re Looking For
  • 3–5+ years of professional frontend engineering experience, building user-facing web applications at scale using JavaScript/TypeScript.
  • Prior experience as a contractor embedded in product teams, with a track record of ramping quickly and delivering value in a short timeframe.
  • Able to independently manage your given task list pipeline as well as communicating your progress throughout the task life cycle.
  • Strong experience with component-based frontend architectures, ideally including Lit or similar web component frameworks (React/Vue/Svelte experience with willingness to learn Lit is acceptable).
  • Solid understanding of modern web fundamentals: semantic HTML, CSS, accessibility best practices, performance optimizations, and responsive layouts.
  • Experience integrating with GraphQL and/or REST APIs, and working effectively with backend engineers to shape APIs and data contracts.
  • Comfort working within existing large codebases and gradually improving their structure, performance, and reliability over time (including CI checks, linting, and testing).
  • Strong ownership mindset: you can take a loosely defined problem, clarify it with stakeholders, ship a well-engineered solution, and iterate based on feedback.
  • Effective communication skills in a remote, cross-functional environment; you’re comfortable collaborating across time zones and functions (PM, Design, Data Science, Safety, etc.).


Nice to Have:

  • Previous experience working on moderation, trust & safety, or admin tools.
  • Familiarity with stack (Lit, GraphQL, SSR, Drone CI, Snoodev), or prior experience on medium-to-large SPAs migrating from legacy to modern stacks.
  • Experience with performance tuning for data-heavy dashboards or queues (e.g., optimizing list rendering, request batching, caching strategies).

🌍 We're Hiring: Senior Frontend Engineer @ Hygo

Hygo reaches 250 million people every month with original video content built around the things people are passionate about. We're not a tech company that dabbles in media — we're a global media company where the product experience is part of the content.

We're looking for a Senior Frontend Engineer who cares deeply about craft — someone who feels the difference between a good UI and a great one, and won't ship until it's the latter.


🎨 What You'll Work On

This is an ownership role — you'll shape the experience millions of creators use to bring their ideas to life.

→ Own the end-to-end user experience across our creator studio, community features, and admin console → Build and extend our V2 design system — a 530+ component library built on Radix UI and Tailwind CSS, documented in Storybook → Ship product features across the full creator journey: onboarding, avatar generation, gallery management, bulk operations, and public profiles → Drive responsive, accessible, and performant UI across every device and screen size → Collaborate closely with design to translate concepts into polished, production-ready interfaces → Maintain visual regression testing with Chromatic and E2E coverage with Playwright


✅ What We're Looking For
  • 4+ years building production React applications
  • Expert-level CSS and Tailwind — complex layouts, animations, and responsive design without reaching for a library
  • Experience building or maintaining a component library or design system at scale
  • Strong product sensibility — you think about user flows, edge cases, and polish, not just implementation
  • Solid Next.js knowledge: App Router, SSR, and server components
  • Experience with Storybook, visual regression testing, or Playwright

🛠 Tech Stack

React 18 · Next.js 14 · TypeScript · Tailwind CSS · Radix UI · Storybook 8 · Playwright · Chromatic · Zustand · React Query


💰 Compensation
  • $120,000 – $140,000 base salary, depending on experience


Why Hygo?

You'll be building the product that creators actually use — not internal dashboards, not a side feature. The studio you own is how people bring their ideas to life at a company reaching a quarter-billion viewers every month. We care about craft, move fast, and give our engineers real ownership over the experience.


Additional Job Application Terms

This job is part of LinkedIn’s Full-Service Hiring beta program. Eligibility is limited to candidates located in and performing services in the United States, excluding those based in Alaska, Hawaii, Nevada, South Carolina, or West Virginia.

jobs.title: Senior Product Designer (Fully Remote)
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga

The Role We Need:



PadSplit is hiring a Senior Product Designer to accelerate growth on the supply side of our two-sided marketplace. This role will shape how property owners discover PadSplit, understand our value, and seamlessly onboard their homes so they can start earning quickly and confidently. You’ll play a central part in designing a frictionless, human, and scalable host experience.



The Person We Are Looking For:



We’re looking for a senior-level designer who blends product intuition, growth-minded experimentation, and deep empathy for users. You thrive in complex systems, move fluidly between big-picture architecture and pixel-level detail, and can clearly tie design decisions to business outcomes. Above all, you are authentic, collaborative, and energized by solving meaningful problems.


Here’s What You’ll Be Doing Day-to-Day:

  • Design Host Funnel - Create and refine pages, flows, and experiments that convert curious owners into successful hosts.

  • Own Onboarding - Simplify property setup, pricing configuration, and listing preparation to ensure hosts launch quickly.

  • Humanize Complexity - Make topics like compliance, property rules, and earnings feel clear, warm, and approachable.

  • Conduct Research - Interview, observe, and gather insights from hosts to translate real needs into stronger UX.

  • Experiment Rapidly - Run landing page tests, funnel optimizations, and A/B experiments with growth and product partners.

  • Collaborate Cross-Functionally - Work closely with PMs, engineers, marketers, and data analysts to deliver cohesive solutions.

  • Craft High-Quality UI - Build responsive, polished interfaces in Figma using thoughtful components and interaction patterns.

  • Build AI-Infused Experiences - Prototype and design intelligent workflows and AI-powered features alongside our ML/AI teams.


Here’s What You’ll Need to Be Successful:

  • Proven Experience - 4+ years designing digital products, ideally with complex acquisition funnels or marketplace environments.

  • Deep UX Skills - Strong research abilities and comfort engaging directly with users to uncover pain points and opportunities.

  • Business Fluency - Ability to connect design decisions to metrics, revenue, and real estate economics like rent, ROI, and occupancy.

  • Workflow Mastery - Experience designing multi-step processes, complex service environments, and edge-case-heavy workflows.

  • UI Excellence - Expertise in Figma and interaction design across web and mobile, with strong systems thinking and component reuse.

  • Data Orientation - Familiarity with A/B testing, experimentation frameworks, and interpreting both qual and quant insights.

  • AI Capability - Demonstrated experience designing AI-powered features and leveraging modern AI tools in your design practice.

  • Authentic Collaboration - A humble, kind, curious, and mission-driven approach that elevates the team and the user experience.


The Interview Process:

  • NO BOTS HERE: your resume will be reviewed by live humans from PeopleOps and the hiring manager, our Head of Design.

  • If we’re interested, the next step would be a video screen with a member of the PeopleOps team for about thirty (30) minutes.

  • If it goes well, the next steps would be a video interview with hiring manager for one (1) hour.

  • If warranted, the final step would be a video panel interview with key stakeholders at PadSplit for one (1) hour.

  • The panel interview will require a candidate to work on a design assessment where you will showcase your design skills to the panel for discussion.

  • If warranted, then we move to offer!



Please note: Although the job posting says it's in Atlanta, Georgia, this is a fully remote position. This is a result of our Applicant Tracking System requiring a location to post the role on LinkedIn.



Notice to Applicants:



PadSplit participates in E-Verify. All new employees are required to complete an I-9 form and be authorized to work in the United States. Employment is contingent upon successful completion of the E-Verify process.



PadSplit is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, or status as a protected veteran.


We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.



Compensation is based on the role's scope, market benchmarks, the person's expertise and experience, and the impact of their contributions to our business goals.

Job Title: Lead Software Engineer

We are seeking an exceptional Development Leader to lead high-performing, full‑stack engineering teams delivering modern, scalable applications. This leader is hands-on, technically credible, and deeply accountable for quality, delivery, and team success.

The ideal candidate has led in a Java Spring Boot microservices backend with a React and Adobe Experience Manager (AEM) front end, and thrives in environments that demand high standards, continuous improvement, and decisive leadership. This role requires someone who challenges the status quo, raises the bar, and builds teams that consistently deliver.


Key Responsibilities

Technical Leadership & Hands-On Contribution
  • Provide hands-on technical leadership across the full stack, including:
  • Java / Spring Boot microservices
  • API design and distributed systems
  • React-based front ends and AEM-driven experiences
  • Review architecture, designs, and code to ensure solutions are scalable, secure, and maintainable
  • Actively participate in design reviews, production issue resolution, and complex technical decisions
  • Set clear technical standards and ensure they are consistently followed across the team


Engineering Excellence & Quality Ownership
  • Be personally accountable for code quality, reliability, and production readiness
  • Ensure strong engineering discipline including:
  • Code reviews and design reviews
  • Automated testing and CI/CD best practices
  • Clear definitions of done and quality gates
  • Proactively identify and address technical debt before it impacts delivery or stability
  • Establish a culture where quality is owned by the team, not inspected in later


People Leadership & Team Success
  • Lead, coach, and develop engineers across levels, building high‑trust, high‑performance teams
  • Set clear expectations, provide direct feedback, and hold the team accountable for results
  • Identify skill gaps and invest in growth through mentoring, learning, and stretch opportunities
  • Create an environment where engineers feel empowered, challenged, and supported


Delivery & Accountability
  • Own delivery outcomes for your teams — commitments, timelines, and results matter
  • Partner closely with Product Owners, Architects, and stakeholders to translate strategy into execution
  • Drive predictable, high‑quality delivery while balancing speed, scope, and sustainability
  • Surface risks early and take decisive action to keep teams on track


Change Agent & Status Quo Challenger
  • Challenge outdated processes, inefficient practices, and unnecessary complexity
  • Drive continuous improvement in how the team plans, builds, tests, and delivers software
  • Encourage healthy debate and data‑driven decision making
  • Lead by example — modeling ownership, transparency, and bias for action


Required Qualifications
  • 8+ years of professional software development experience

  • Strong hands‑on experience with:
  • Java, Spring Boot, and microservices architectures
  • React front-end development
  • Adobe Experience Manager (AEM)
  • Proven track record of delivering high‑quality software at scale
  • Experience holding teams accountable while maintaining strong morale and trust


Preferred Qualifications

What Success Looks Like
  • Teams consistently deliver high-quality, reliable software
  • Engineers are engaged, growing, and proud of what they build
  • Quality issues and rework decline over time
  • Technical debt is actively managed, not ignored
  • The team continuously improves — not because they are told to, but because the leader expects it

If you meet these qualifications and are excited about leveraging your skills in a collaborative environment, we would love to hear from you!

StratusGrid delivers a high-trust, high-competence customer experience around cloud optimization, and we’re productizing that expertise into our agentic platform, Stratusphere. This role is for a versatile software engineer who can help build the core software platform, the backend infrastructure running agents, and the agents and tooling that discovers, contextualizes, plans, and executes work for our customers.


You will work closely with our customer success and human in the loop teams, as well as directly with customers, to rapidly test, implement, and mature solutions that make our system increasingly autonomous. Problems we are solving range from the discovery and contextualization of complex cost inefficiencies in cloud infrastructure, to designing and implementing web interfaces that enable customers, to making agents and tooling that can plan and execute changes inside of complex cloud environments. To do this, you will be using agent-first development practices in a product that was designed to be developed agent first, bringing critical judgement, maintainability, and design expertise to the rapid AI development cycle.


Responsibilities:
  • Agent-First Software Development: Actively utilize and oversee AI agents to rapidly test, implement, and mature software solutions across the web platform, backend services, infrastructure, data pipelines, and agent tooling that make up the Stratusphere platform.
  • Engineering Judgment & Scrutiny: Serve as the ultimate source of judgment for AI-generated plans and code contributed to the Stratusphere platform. Scrutinize all agent-driven implementations to ensure they are rigorously tested, scalable, maintainable, and meet StratusGrid's engineering standards.
  • Add to the Foundation: Stay current with the latest methods and best practices for improving velocity, quality, and maintainability with agent-first development approaches. Share knowledge and actively contribute to maturing processes, standards, and tooling to which makes the whole team better.
  • Collaborative System Design: Partner closely with engineering, product, and customer-facing teams to design and evolve the Stratusphere platform, ensuring technical decisions align with strategic goals and customer needs.
  • Agent Improvement Feedback Loop: Improve agentic architecture, tooling, and prompts to solve for patterns in agent errors and blind spots (e.g., missing context, risky sequencing, unclear rollback, incomplete stakeholder info). Propose rubric changes, training examples, and workflow improvements; partner with Product/Human in the Loop teams to measurably improve quality and safety.
  • Cross-Functional Collaboration: Work in high-visibility channels with Engineering, Product, and Customer teams; share context broadly; ask questions early; and support a safe culture where we learn fast without introducing risk through isolation.
  • Execution with Reliability & Urgency: Own work end-to-end with a strong sense of responsibility. Capture commitments, hit deadlines, communicate status proactively, escalate early, and close the loop visibly—no surprises.
  • Product Partnership & Roadmap Input: Partner with Product to test solutions to customer problems and recurring friction, iterate from learnings, and implement features which are based on data and proven results.
  • Operational Excellence & Standards: Follow StratusGrid customer experience standards, change control processes, documentation expectations, and work-system hygiene to ensure consistency, traceability, and scalability.


Requirements:
  • Multi-Language Proficiency: Deep expertise in at least one language (preferably Typescript) combined with proficiency in overseeing agentic delivery of solutions in other languages. Ability to quickly adapt to new languages/frameworks as required.
  • Skillful Tester: Skilled in architecting services to allow for layered testing, and then consistently using testing to enforce quality and maintainability while rapidly developing solutions with AI Agents.
  • Frontend Experience: Experience contributing to frontend applications, especially NextJS or other React frameworks.
  • Familiar with Coding Agents: Familiar with, and excited about using, coding agents such as Claude Code, Codex, and Gemini CLI to rapidly plan, write, and test high-quality and maintainable code.
  • Multifaceted Database Expertise: Experience querying large datasets with tools like AWS Athena or DuckDB, creating applications backed by relational databases like Postgres, and experience working with NoSQL solutions like AWS DynamoDB.
  • CICD Experience: Experience building, maintaining, and using tools like GitHub Actions to test code and catch quality issues early.
  • AWS and IaC Expertise (AWS): Proven ability to build and change infrastructure in production AWS environments using infrastructure code, including container environments and cloud native services like Lambda, S3, and CloudFront.
  • Observability: Experience integrating observability tooling into software and using it to quickly troubleshoot complex problems and improve system performance.
  • Excellent Communication: Exceptional written and verbal communication, able to translate complex technical topics into decision-ready narratives for technical teammates and business stakeholders, with clarity, completeness, and empathy. Ability to listen to customer concerns and turn them into actionable solutions.
  • Problem Solving Mindset: Proven abilit

jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer, Frontend (Advanced Trade)
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga

Ready to be pushed beyond what you think you’re capable of?

At Coinbase, our mission is to increase economic freedom in the world. It’s a massive, ambitious opportunity that demands the best of us, every day, as we build the emerging onchain platform — and with it, the future global financial system.

To achieve our mission, we’re seeking a very specific candidate. We want someone who is passionate about our mission and who believes in the power of crypto and blockchain technology to update the financial system. We want someone who is eager to leave their mark on the world, who relishes the pressure and privilege of working with high caliber colleagues, and who actively seeks feedback to keep leveling up. We want someone who will run towards, not away from, solving the company’s hardest problems.

Our work culture is intense and isn’t for everyone. But if you want to build the future alongside others who excel in their disciplines and expect the same from you, there’s no better place to be.

While many roles at Coinbase are remote-first, we are not remote-only. In-person participation is required throughout the year. Team and company-wide offsites are held multiple times annually to foster collaboration, connection, and alignment. Attendance is expected and fully supported.

Coinbase Advanced is our advanced trading platform, intended for a more experienced trader. It offers a secure and easy way to buy, sell, and trade digital assets online across various trading pairs.

We are looking for a Senior Software Engineer to lead the front-end technical strategy for this platform. This role is designed for a technical leader who can drive significant performance improvements across our web and mobile clients. This is a rare opportunity to work at the intersection of crypto, trading infrastructure, and consumer product — tackling exciting engineering challenges while contributing directly to Coinbase’s core business

What you’ll be doing:
  • Collaborate with Product and Engineering leadership to define and execute a technical roadmap for a reliable, scalable trading platform
  • Design and implement scalable, maintainable, and high-performance user interfaces using modern frontend technologies like React and TypeScript.
  • Collaborate with backend engineers, designers, product managers, and senior leadership to turn product and technical visions into tangible roadmaps.
  • Anchor complex projects and ensure high standards for code quality and system design

What we look for in you:
  • You have at least 5 years of experience in software engineering.
  • Experience optimizing frontend performance for low-latency, high-reliability systems.
  • Strong communication skills, with the ability to translate complex technical challenges into actionable plans for stakeholders
  • Demonstrates the ability to responsibly use generative AI tools and copilots (e.g., Claude, LibreChat, Gemini, Glean) in daily workflows, continuously learn as tools evolve, and apply human-in-the-loop practices to deliver business-ready outputs and drive measurable improvements in efficiency, cost, and quality.

Nice to haves:
  • You have gone through a rapid growth in your company (from 10 to 100s of engineers)
  • You have experience building a low latency trading system
  • You have experience with Blockchains (such as Bitcoin, Ethereum etc.)
  • Crypto-forward experience, including familiarity with onchain activity such as interacting with Ethereum addresses, using ENS, and engaging with dApps or blockchain-based services.

    Job# P76257

Pay Transparency Notice: Depending on your work location, the target annual base salary for this position can range as detailed below. Total compensation may also include equity and bonus eligibility and benefits (including medical, dental, vision and 401(k)).

Annual base salary range (excluding equity and bonus):: $186,065 USD - $218,900 USD

Please be advised that each candidate may submit a maximum of four applications within any 30-day period. We encourage you to carefully evaluate how your skills and interests align with Coinbase's roles before applying.

Commitment to Equal Opportunity

Coinbase is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, creed, gender, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, sex, gender expression or identity, sexual orientation or any other basis protected by applicable law. Coinbase will also consider for employment qualified applicants with criminal histories in a manner consistent with applicable federal, state and local law. For US applicants, you may view the Employee Rights and the Know Your Rights notices by clicking on their corresponding links. Additionally, Coinbase participates in the E-Verify program in certain locations, as required by law.

Coinbase is also committed to providing reasonable accommodations to individuals with disabilities. If you need a reasonable accommodation because of a disability for any part of the employment process, please contact us at accommodations[at]coinbase.com to let us know the nature of your request and your contact information. For quick access to screen reading technology compatible with this site click here to download a free compatible screen reader (free step by step tutorial can be found here).

Global Data Privacy Notice for Job Candidates and Applicants

Depending on your location, the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) and California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) may regulate the way we manage the data of job applicants. Our full notice outlining how data will be processed as part of the application procedure for applicable locations is available here. By submitting your application, you are agreeing to our use and processing of your data as required. For US applicants only, by submitting your application you are agreeing to arbitration of disputes as outlined here.

AI Disclosure

For select roles, Coinbase is piloting an AI tool based on machine learning technologies to conduct initial screening interviews to qualified applicants. The tool simulates realistic interview scenarios and engages in dynamic conversation. A human recruiter will review your interview responses, provided in the form of a voice recording and/or transcript, to assess them against the qualifications and characteristics outlined in the job

jobs.title: Software Engineer
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga

Final-stage candidates will be asked to travel to our Boca Raton, Florida office. Team members may also be asked to travel 1–2 times per year for executive team meetings. Due to funding requirements, we are only able to hire employees based in the United States.


RecruiterPM is a growing SaaS platform helping recruiting firms, staffing agencies, and employers streamline hiring through one connected platform that combines Applicant Tracking System (ATS), CRM, and Project Management capabilities into a single experience.


As we continue to grow, we are investing heavily in the future of search, automation, and AI across the platform. We are looking for a hands-on engineer who can help us modernize the product, improve performance, and build practical AI-powered features that create real value for users.


This is an opportunity for someone who enjoys building across the stack, improving legacy functionality, and helping shape where a SaaS platform should be heading in 2027.


What You’ll Do
  • Build and enhance AI-powered product features across the RecruiterPM platform
  • Help design and implement RAG-based experiences, intelligent search workflows, and agentic AI features for end users
  • Build integrations and workflows using modern LLM platforms, including Claude-based MCPs and related AI tooling
  • Improve OpenSearch relevance and performance across structured, keyword, and AI-assisted search experiences
  • Optimize MySQL queries, indexes, execution plans, and schema design to improve speed and scalability
  • Help improve our CI/CD pipelines, deployment workflows, and release processes for faster and safer product delivery
  • Support DevOps-oriented engineering work, including build pipelines, testing automation, environment consistency, and production deployment reliability
  • Partner on infrastructure and deployment improvements across application performance, monitoring, and scalability
  • Develop and maintain full-stack product functionality using PHP, Laravel, MySQL, Linux, Vue.js, and JavaScript
  • Partner with product and leadership to identify high-impact technical improvements and bring them to life
  • Help evolve our platform architecture so it can scale with product growth and future AI initiatives


What We’re Looking For
  • 4+ years of software engineering experience, ideally in a SaaS product environment
  • Strong experience with PHP and Laravel
  • Solid experience writing and optimizing complex MySQL queries
  • Experience working with search technologies such as OpenSearch or Elasticsearch
  • Hands-on experience building or integrating AI-powered product features using LLMs, vector search, embeddings, RAG, or workflow automation
  • Ability to work across the stack, including frontend work in JavaScript, HTML, CSS, and Vue.js or similar frameworks
  • Experience working with CI/CD pipelines, deployment workflows, and modern software release practices
  • Familiarity with DevOps concepts such as build automation, testing pipelines, environment management, logging, monitoring, and production troubleshooting
  • Comfortable working in Linux-based environments
  • Strong problem-solving skills and the ability to balance speed, quality, and scalability in a growing startup


Nice to Have
  • Experience building agentic AI workflows or AI copilots inside SaaS platforms
  • Familiarity with Claude, MCPs, prompt orchestration, or related AI application patterns
  • Experience with Docker, GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, Bitbucket Pipelines, Jenkins, or similar CI/CD tooling
  • Familiarity with cloud infrastructure, server management, and deployment automation
  • Experience supporting production SaaS environments with observability and incident troubleshooting
  • Experience in ATS, CRM, HR tech, recruiting tech, or workflow-heavy SaaS platforms
  • Familiarity with DigitalOcean or similar cloud environments
  • Experience with APIs, automation tools, and third-party integrations
  • Interest in helping shape technical direction as the engineering team grows


Why Join Us
  • Help build the future of AI, search, and workflow automation in recruiting software
  • Work directly with company founders and have real influence on product direction
  • Join at a stage where your work can meaningfully shape the platform’s evolution
  • Remote work within the U.S.
  • Opportunity for long-term growth as the company scales

jobs.title: Lead Software Engineer
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga

Company Description

Ten Mile Square Technologies is a high-end technology consulting firm based in the Northern Virginia area. Our customers routinely call upon us to solve some of the largest scale and hardest problems in computer science and software development. If you have solid grounding in software engineering, continuous delivery, and cloud architecture, and are looking for a career opportunity to grow and leverage those skills to the fullest while working on cutting edge technologies – Ten Mile Square is the place to be!


Job Description


OVERVIEW

Ten Mile Square is looking for a talented and experienced Lead Software Engineer to lead a remote-first team of talented full-stack software developers. The successful candidate will have a history of hands-on leadership and expertise with software development methods, technologies, and tooling. The initial assignment is greenfield development of a loan origination system for an established financial institution. You will work with our technology leadership, partners, and engineers to deliver high velocity high value solutions to our client.


Salary Range (Base + Standard Bonus): $172,500 ($150K + $22,500


JOB RESPONSIBILITIES
  • Lead a team of software engineers, software engineer in test automation (SDET), and DevOps
  • Architect, design, and build a telephony integration system using Node, Apollo GraphQL, Angular or React, and a suite of AWS components including but not limited to EKS, Lambda, S3, MSK, and Aurora. The components are primarily backend systems, but some frontend development will be needed.
  • Design and implement GraphQL subgraphs and participate in a federated graph schema community of practice
  • Define and enforce engineering best practices around code quality, testing strategies, CI/CD pipelines, and deployment workflows on AWS
  • Contribute as member of an Agile team and promote Agile practices
  • Work with client executives and product team to translate business needs into requirements, epics, stories, and tasks
  • Work with the client product team to groom the backlog, plan workstreams, plan sprints, and assign sprints and tasks to software engineers
  • Work with principal engineers and the product team to help determine functional requirements for the system.
  • Mentor developers on the team.
  • Ensure technical documentation is of quality and accessible to technology team.
  • Proactively collaborate with the Ten Mile team on development and knowledge sharing.
  • Represent yourself and Ten Mile Square with integrity, expertise, and value.
  • Maintain the confidentiality of our clients on an ongoing basis.


Qualifications
  • 2 years of experience as a tech lead
  • 3 years of experience as a senior software engineer
  • 7+ years of software development experience including 3 years of TypeScript and Node
  • 2 years of experience working with GraphQL
  • Expertise in developing and deploying on AWS
  • Expertise in SQL
  • Working knowledge of at least one of the following frontend JavaScript frameworks: Angular, Ionic, React, Vue
  • Full application stack development experience: front-end to persistence
  • Full software lifecycle participation: requirements to post-implementation
  • Linux command line

More importantly,
  • Self-motivated to PROACTIVELY identify and solve problems
  • Be passionate about what you do
  • Demonstrated ability to learn new technologies and concepts quickly
  • Detail-oriented
  • High integrity and quality of work ethic
  • Excellent communication skills – within team and with clients


Additional Information

Ten Mile Square Technologies benefits package currently includes:
  • 100% company paid medical, dental, and vision insurance (including family)
  • 100% company paid individual short- and long- term disability
  • 3% company contribution to 401(k)
  • 15 days paid time off per year
  • 10 training days per year
  • 8 company holidays
  • Individual performance-based quarterly cash incentive
  • Combined individual and company based annual cash incentive
  • Public transit reimbursement

Additional Details:
  • Job Type: Full-time, not C2C
  • Job Location: Remote

CGS is seeking a Senior UI/UX Designer to join our team supporting a wide-ranging technical support initiative for a large Federal agency.

CGS brings motivated, highly skilled, and creative people together to solve the government’s most dynamic problems with cutting-edge technology. To carry out our mission, we are seeking candidates who are excited to contribute to government innovation, appreciate collaboration, and can anticipate the needs of others. Here at CGS, we offer an environment in which our employees feel supported, and we encourage professional growth through various learning opportunities.

Skills and attributes for success:

  • Lead full-scale UX design efforts to include research, blueprinting, and evaluating existing systems
  • Manage the planning, design, and implementation of multiple sites and applications across multiple clients simultaneously in a fast-paced environment
  • Lead all phases of user research and analysis needed to inform the creation of highly usable web pages, application interfaces, and other dynamic solutions
  • Collaborate with federal practice engineers and federal clients to define, design, and implement innovative, beautiful, intuitive solutions for use by our federal customer and their stakeholders
  • Create wireframes, storyboards, and site maps to effectively communicate interaction and design ideas for websites and applications
  • Create scalable design resources to aid in project collaboration and the expansion of ECS creative services




Qualifications:
  • Bachelor's Degree
  • Must be able to obtain a Public Trust
  • Strong UX design experience
  • 10+ years combined professional design experience (UCD, UI/UX design)
  • Strong online portfolio that showcases the candidate’s ability to make research-driven decisions in the design of responsive web and mobile applications
  • Results-oriented problem solver with high standards for quality, accuracy, attention to detail, and overall excellence
  • Expertise in user research methodologies, user centered design principles and frameworks, and user interface design standards
  • Advanced understanding of user personas, user flows, affinity mapping, and other research tools
  • Strong understanding of the life cycle process of website development (discovery, planning, design, requirements, coding, testing, and user testing and evaluation)
  • Experience evaluating existing systems and processes to identify UX issues and develop UX recommendations
  • Experience designing web and mobile applications that are compliant with 508 and US Web Design System (USWDS) standards
  • Experience prioritizing features while accounting for user goals and business requirements
  • Experience carrying designs from start to finish, from wireframes to delivery of final high-fidelity UI mockups
  • Experience establishing and maintaining rapid customer feedback loops to inform design at critical stages
  • Self-starter, motivated, confident and has ability to work independently as well as in a team environment
  • Success on projects designed from scratch as well as redesigns for established platforms or products
  • Experience mentoring/leading junior designers
  • Advanced coding knowledge (HTML, CSS, and JS)
  • Advanced proficiency in Adobe xD, InDesign, Illustrator, and Photoshop




Ideally, you will also have:
  • Excellent interpersonal and client focused skills— interacts well with all levels of staff and partners with a positive and enthusiastic attitude
  • Experience using Agile methodology to manage projects
  • Experience creating and modifying data visualizations
  • Proficient in Microsoft Office Suite




Our Commitment:

Contact Government Services (CGS) strives to simplify and enhance government bureaucracy through the optimization of human, technical, and financial resources. We combine cutting-edge technology with world-class personnel to deliver customized solutions that fit our client’s specific needs. jobs.title: Full Stack Engineer
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga

Intellicheck - Full Stack Engineer

March 2026

Salary: $85K - $125K

Remote


Overview


Intellicheck delivers real-time, authoritative identity verification by analyzing DMV-issued ID data with a single scan to detect fake or altered IDs. Our platform minimizes false positives while ensuring a seamless, trusted experience for users, supporting both in-person and digital transactions. We verify nearly 100 million IDs annually for major banks, credit card issuers, title insurance companies, and law enforcement agencies across North America. As a hardware vendor-neutral, omnichannel SaaS solution, Intellicheck works seamlessly with your existing mobile devices and scanners to provide reliable identity verification at scale.


We are seeking a highly skilled and experienced Full Stack Engineer (Level II) with at least 5 years of experience. The Full Stack Software Engineer II position is a mid-level engineering position that requires experience building production web and mobile applications using modern cloud technologies. As a Full Stack Software Engineer, you will be responsible for designing, developing, and deploying Intellicheck’s family of ID Verification technology solutions including API, Mobile, Web and native applications. Additionally, you will be expected to mentor team members and contractors working closely with stakeholders to ensure timelines are met.


The ideal candidate will have a strong background with all aspects of software development and be comfortable working in a SCRUM environment. Clear communication skills are a must. This position is fully remote. In this role, you will:
  • Design, develop, and maintain scalable web applications using Python (backend) and React with TypeScript (frontend) using modern AI code generation tools.
  • Develop RESTful APIs using the FastAPI framework.
  • Create Elasticsearch aggregation queries, data exports and data visualizations.
  • Work with the data science teams to containerize machine learning models for deployment in a Kubernetes cluster
  • Write clean, maintainable, and well-documented code.
  • Ensure compatibility across different browsers and devices.
  • Collaborate closely with product and engineering teams.
  • Conduct code reviews to maintain the highest code quality.
  • Participate in Scrum Ceremonies and ensure inexperienced team members understand their responsibilities and how to deliver on-time.
  • Stay current with the latest technologies and industry trends to ensure continuous improvement and innovation.
  • Take full ownership of the design, development and delivery of dynamic, user responsive user interfaces and the API’s necessary to drive them.
  • Perform thorough testing on production release days to ensure the application meets quality and performance metrics.
  • Work alongside the QA team to ensure deliverables meeting required functionality


Qualified candidates will have a Bachelor’s Degree in Computer Science, Engineering, IT or other related field along with proven experience as a Software Engineer with a focus on Python (backend) and React with TypeScript (frontend), strong proficiency in building applications using Python and working with relational and/or SQL databases, expertise in React, React Native and Typescript for creating dynamic and responsive web and mobile user interface and experience with Bootstrap and ReactBootstrap CSS frameworks.


Must have a solid understanding of software development principles, RESTful API design, and web security, familiarity with frontend state management libraries such as Redux, experience with DevOps practices, CI/CD pipelines, and containerization (Docker) and experience with ETL processes and transforming data from disparate sources. Strong problem-solving skills and attention to detail are a must.


We offer a collaborative work environment, competitive salary (with bonus upside), and comprehensive benefits for you and your family.


Intellicheck, Inc. provides equal employment opportunities to current and prospective employees, without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, ancestry, sexual orientation, age, pregnancy, disability, handicap, citizenship, veteran or marital status, or any other legally recognized status entitled to protection under federal, state, or local anti-discrimination laws.


This is a fully remote role.


Qualified candidates should send a resume.

Sr. Appian Developer

Remote | CST or EST time zones (preferred)

Contract through the end of the year.


Our client is a highly innovative organization that is pushing the boundaries of what Appian can do. They are deep into newer Appian capabilities like GenAI and Process HQ, actively experimenting in sandbox and beta environments, and using the platform across multiple parts of the business. This is not a maintenance role. They are looking for a Senior Appian Architect who can help shape how the platform is used, what’s possible with it, and where it goes next.


What You’ll Do:
  • As the senior technical resource for Appian, you will be responsible for the design, development, and ongoing evolution of business-critical solutions on the Appian platform.
  • Architect, design, and build custom Appian applications using a mix of out-of-the-box functionality and custom components
  • Serve as the Appian “feature expert,” advising the business on what Appian can do today and how to best leverage new and emerging capabilities
  • Lead and influence solution design with scalability, performance, and long-term growth in mind
  • Implement enhancements and new functionality across existing Appian applications
  • Work heavily with integrations, including complex, ground-up integrations (not just basic connectors)

  • Provide technical leadership, coaching, and guidance to Appian developers and users
  • Review, audit, and QA work delivered by internal teams and external vendor resources
  • Actively participate in sandbox and beta environments to evaluate and adopt new Appian features


What We’re Looking For
  • This role requires someone senior enough to influence direction and make recommendations, not just execute against predefined tickets.
  • 4+ years of hands-on Appian development and architecture experience
  • Deep, hands‑on Process HQ expertise who can actively drive and advance the company’s capabilities in this Appian space

  • Experience building complex integrations, including API-based integrations from the ground up
  • Experience integrating Appian with platforms like Salesforce is a major plus
  • Appian Certified Lead Developer (or similar) which shows commitment to the platform and personal growth
  • Comfortable working directly with business users and stakeholders
  • Working knowledge of application servers, web servers, and operating systems
  • Curious, innovative, and forward-thinking mindset with a willingness to experiment and push the platform


Why This Role Stands Out
  • Appian is used broadly across the organization, not in isolated pockets
  • The team actively adopts new Appian features early and encourages experimentation
  • You’ll have real influence over platform direction and solution design
  • This is a product and solution oriented environment, not a rigid government-style implementation

jobs.title: JotForm Specialist
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga

JotForm Conditional Logic Configuration


Project Overview

I am seeking a specialist with experience building structured inquiry forms using JotForm conditional logic.


The form has already been created, and the question structure is complete. The remaining task is to configure and troubleshoot the conditional logic so that the form routes inquiries correctly based on the requested service.


This will be the primary client intake form for my business website.


Estimated scope: 2–4 hours of work for someone experienced with JotForm logic workflows.


Compensation: Fixed project fee is $200.



Objective

Configure the form so that client inquiries are automatically routed to the appropriate service area and display only the questions relevant to that service.

The form structure and routing logic have already been designed. The goal is simply to implement the logic correctly.


Form Structure

Section 1 — Contact Information

Fields include:
  • Name
  • Email
  • Organization (optional)
  • Phone (optional)


How did you hear about Flair for Writing?

Dropdown options:
  • LinkedIn post
  • LinkedIn group
  • LinkedIn article
  • Google search
  • Website search
  • Newsletter (The Sanctified Script)
  • Podcast (The Sanctified Script)
  • Returning client
  • Referral
  • Other

If Referral is selected, display an additional field:

Who referred you?


Section 2 — Service Selection (Primary Routing Field)

Field label:

Which service are you seeking?


Dropdown options:

• Faith-Based Content Creation

• Professional Editing Services

• Caregiver Dissertation Support

• Technical Writing & Documentation

• General Inquiry


This field determines all subsequent conditional logic.



Section 3 — Department Questions

When a service area is selected, the form should display only the questions associated with that department.


Faith-Based Content Creation

Fields:

Type of content

Dropdown:
  • Newsletter
  • Bible study
  • Devotional
  • Podcast script
  • Blog/article
  • Book project
  • Other


Additional fields:

Audience description

Estimated length

Timeline

Budget range



Professional Editing Services

Fields may include:
  • Type of manuscript
  • Editing level required
  • Estimated word count
  • Timeline
  • Budget range



Caregiver Dissertation Support

Fields may include:
  • Stage of dissertation
  • Type of assistance needed
  • Timeline
  • Budget range



Technical Writing & Documentation

Fields may include:
  • Type of documentation
  • Industry
  • Project scope
  • Timeline
  • Budget range



Conditional Logic Requirements

The form should:
  • Display department-specific questions based on Service Selection
  • Display referral field only when Referral is selected
  • Display appropriate budget dropdowns depending on service type
  • Route responses to the correct email inbox based on service selection



Security Requirement

For security reasons:
  • WordPress access will not be provided
  • Work will be completed within JotForm only


The specialist will gain access to a duplicate of the form via JotForm’s collaborator feature.

Once the logic is functioning correctly, the updated form will replace the existing embed code on the website.



Deliverables

The completed work should include:

• Fully functioning conditional logic

• Proper question routing based on service selection

• Clean logic workflow without errors

• Email routing is functioning correctly



What to Incl

jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga

We believe communication belongs to everyone. We exist to democratize phone service.  TextNow is evolving the way the world connects and that's because we're made up of people with curious minds who bring an optimistic, yet critical lens into the work we do.   We're the largest provider of free phone service in the nation. And we're just getting started.

Join us in our mission to break down barriers to communication and free the flow of conversation for people everywhere.

TextNow is looking for an experienced Senior Web Developer to play a key role in the development of our voice and messaging platform on Web. You will be responsible for building high-performance, scalable, and feature-rich mobile applications that integrate voice, video, and messaging capabilities. You will work alongside a talented team of developers, product managers, and UX/UI designers to create intuitive and user-centric experiences.


What You'll Do
  • Design, develop, and maintain a high-quality Web application focused on voice and messaging features (voice calling, chat, real-time notifications, etc.).

jobs.title: Software Engineer II
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga

Collette is seeking a Software Engineer II to join our growing Technology Team. This is a remote role.

About Collette: Let Us Show You the World

There has never been a better time to be in the travel industry. See the world, connect with others, and experience the immersive benefits of one of the trendiest industries when you join the Collette team! As the longest-running tour operator in North America, our family-owned business leads the industry in innovation and expertise. Collette’s passionate team works hard to fulfill travel dreams and is always looking for talent that will help to shape the company’s future. Being part of the Collette team means having a willingness to grow, a desire to learn and ask questions, and a zest for seeing the world. From giving back to local communities to creating incredible experiences for a world of travelers and being part of a value-oriented team that cares for one another – Collette is all about the people. So, what are you waiting for? Your journey starts here.


Job Summary:

At Collette, we're at the beginning of our journey transitioning from a monolithic application stack to a distributed, microservice-based architecture. As a leader in guided travel, we're dedicated to crafting exceptional experiences for our travelers. We are seeking a Software Engineer to provide design and implementation of our next generation customer web product. Utilizing a modern cloud-based infrastructure and catering to hundreds of thousands of travelers worldwide. This role requires someone willing to work full stack in a microservices architecture with a front-end built in React and Next.js. A successful candidate will have a broad skillset covering front-end and back-end technologies.


Primary Functions:
  • Design and develop back-end services in C#, Docker, Kubernetes, and SQL Server and front-end applications in React, Next.js and Node.js.
  • Ensure quality via automated testing.
  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams including product managers, designers, and other engineers to deliver scalable and reliable software solutions.
  • Stay updated on emerging technologies and industry trends, continuously enhancing your skills and knowledge to drive innovation within the team.


Knowledge & Skills:
  • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science (preferred) or related field.
  • Understanding of software engineering principles and practices.
  • Experience in front-end technologies including React, Next.js and Node.js.
  • Experience in API design and development.
  • Experience in relational databases, preferably SQL Server.
  • Excellent problem-solving skills and ability to thrive in a fast-paced, collaborative environment.
  • Strong communication skills with the ability to effectively communicate technical concepts to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
  • Self-starter with a bias for action, a passion for innovation and continuous learning.


Compensation & Benefits

We believe in taking care of our team—inside and outside of work.


The base salary range for this position is $80,000 - $140,000.


What We Offer:
  • Health & Wellness: Medical, Dental, and Vision coverage—plus a Peloton One Membership to keep you moving
  • Time Off: Generous PTO, paid holidays, and your birthday!
  • Volunteer Time: Paid hours to give back to causes you care about
  • Financial Security: 401(k) with company match
  • Family Support: Paid parental leave, paid bereavement leave and access to our Employee Assistance Program
  • Career Growth: Tuition reimbursement to help you level up your skills
  • Exclusive travel discounts, incentives and more!


Above all, we're seeking an individual who is eager to immerse themselves in our business, embrace emerging technologies, and drive impactful change within our organization.

Senior Frontend Engineer (GraphQL Focus)


We’re looking for a highly skilled Senior Frontend Engineer with deep GraphQL expertise to help design, build, and scale modern, user‑centric web applications.
